title: Accessory layer — 5 starter accessories on named mount sockets, with soft-attached tagging for spring sway
intent: S101's generator has zero accessories — the bomber is body parts and nothing else. This story adds the accessory layer that lives in `characters-and-visual.md` §2.1: a recipe carries 0..3 accessories from a closed enum (antennae, visor, backpack, cap, fins). Each accessory is a small mesh tree attached to a named mount socket on the body (head crown, head eyes, torso back, torso sides). Accessories are tagged `soft-attached` so the spring-sway system from GDP-2026-05-20-009 can drive their secondary motion. Pure recipe extension — no new texturing layer, no new shader, no new palette channel (accessories paint from existing channels).
rationale: Source: `docs/game-design/characters-and-visual.md` §2.1 (recipe.accessories field) + §6.1 (accessory selection from a 5-item enum). Accessories are the largest single contributor to silhouette variety: an antenna stick changes the head silhouette far more than a 10% headSize bump does. They're also the lever the cosmetic-unlock system (`gdd.md` Cosmetic unlocks) acts on — unlocks add accessories to the recipe pool. Without them, the cosmetic-unlock plan has nothing to unlock.
acceptanceHints: Depends on GDP-2026-05-21-001 (mount sockets live on the multi-mesh node tree — the merged-geometry v0 has no parent nodes to attach to).
Mount sockets — Object3D children of body meshes, placed at well-known anatomical positions. Generator creates them on every bomber whether or not any accessory uses them:
head.crown — top of the head mesh, used by antennae + cap
head.eyes — front-centre of the head at eye height, used by visor
head.ear.l / head.ear.r — sides of the head, reserved for future ear-fin / earphone accessories
torso.back — back of the torso, used by backpack
torso.side.l / torso.side.r — sides of the torso, used by fins
Naming convention identical to limb pivots from GDP-2026-05-21-001: lowercase, dot-suffix.
5 starter accessories (each is a small mesh subtree, parented to its mount socket):

ANTENNAE — 1 or 2 thin cylinders (deterministic-by-seed) on head.crown, each topped with a small sphere painted palette.accent (emissive). Tagged soft-attached so the spring-sway system bobs them when the head moves. Cylinder length ~0.15 of headSize.
VISOR — a thin curved box (or low-res rounded box) across head.eyes, painted palette.helmet. Slightly translucent (opacity ~0.4) via material variant — the eye dots underneath read through it.

BACKPACK — a rounded box at torso.back, painted palette.torso × 0.85. Size ~0.4 × 0.35 × 0.15. NOT tagged soft-attached (backpacks don't sway visibly). Reserved use: when the bomber holds a bomb, the bomb mesh visually 'comes from' the backpack region.
CAP — a cone (or hemisphere + brim) on head.crown, painted palette.helmet. Brim painted palette.head × 0.7 for two-tone read.
FINS — 2 flat triangular meshes on torso.side.l and torso.side.r, painted palette.torsoAccent. Each fin ~0.25 × 0.3 thin. Tagged soft-attached (they wobble during walk).
Each accessory builder is its own pure function under examples/procbomber-bench/src/generators/accessories/<name>.ts returning { root: Object3D, parts: Mesh[] }.
Recipe surface: `recipe.accessories: ('antennae'|'visor'|'backpack'|'cap'|'fins')[]` — 0..3 items, no duplicates, no nulls. Schema validator enforces the cardinality + enum.

Mounting rule: each accessory declares its required mount socket(s) in a manifest. The generator looks up the socket on the bomber's tree and parents the accessory's root Object3D under it. If a recipe omits accessories, no socket extras are mounted (sockets always exist as empty pivots — that's fine, they cost ~nothing).

Default selection from seed: when `recipe.accessories` is unspecified, the generator picks 0..3 (heavily weighted toward 1..2) from a seed-derived index sequence. Rule: take seed bits 8..15, mod 4 → count (0/1/2/3 with weights 1/3/3/2); take subsequent 3-bit chunks, mod 5 → each accessory index, dedup, until count is reached.

Soft-attached tag: each mesh tagged 'soft-attached' (antennae cylinders, fin meshes) gets a small ECS metadata flag visible to the spring-sway-system from GDP-2026-05-20-009. Implementation: the accessory builders push the mesh handles into a `SoftAttachedNodes` array on the bomber entity's runtime metadata; the spring-sway system reads it and applies its 2-tap spring.

Palette interaction: each accessory paints from the existing 8-channel palette (helmet for cap/visor, torso for backpack, accent for antenna tip, torsoAccent for fins) — no new colour channels. Cosmetic unlocks acting on accessories use the per-channel override from GDP-2026-05-21-003 (e.g. unlock paints just the cap to gold without touching the rest of the bomber).

Bench UI: a new 'Accessories' section with 5 checkboxes (antennae / visor / backpack / cap / fins). Selecting a 4th forces deselection of the oldest (cap at 3). Reroll button respects the count distribution above.
Determinism: 200 random seeds → 200 accessory selections → every selection has 0..3 unique items from the enum. Same seed twice → bit-identical selection + accessory mesh trees.

Visual diversity test: with 5 accessories × 0..3 cardinality and 8 palette families, the bench should be able to produce 50+ visibly distinct bombers in a row before a duplicate-feeling silhouette appears. Manual QA bar; documented in bench README.

Performance: each accessory adds 1..3 meshes. Worst case: 3 accessories × 3 meshes = 9 extra meshes per bomber. At 4 bombers, 36 extra draw calls. Combined with the body's 10 meshes = 46 per bomber = 184 total at 4 bombers + arena. Within the 200 draw-call budget from visual-style.md §11; if it tips over, batching policy from S70 InstancedMesh applies.

Out of scope (explicit): accessory variants (large vs small backpack, single vs double antenna), per-accessory colour overrides outside the existing palette, gameplay-affecting accessories (e.g. 'helmet gives blast resistance' — that contradicts the cosmetic-only rule in gdd.md Cosmetic unlocks). Accessory unlocks: defer to the cosmetic-unlock proposal once persistent profile lands.
